---
title: "FLicc: Length-based multi-gear catch-curve model"
output:
   rmarkdown::html_document:
    self_contained: true
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{FLicc workflow}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  \usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.width = 6,
  fig.height = 4
)

```

# Introduction

`FLicc` provides an FLR-compatible implementation of an equilibrium length-based catch-curve model for multi-gear fisheries.

This vignette introduces the model assumptions and walks through a full workflow, explaining what each step does.

# Model formulation

## Population-at-length

Fishing mortality by gear:
$$
F_g(L) = F_g \cdot S_g(L)
$$

Total mortality:
$$
Z(L) = M(L) + \sum_g F_g S_g(L)
$$

## Selectivity functions

### Logistic
$$
S(L) = \frac{1}{1 + \exp\left(-\frac{L - L_{50}}{\delta}\right)}
$$

### Double-sided normal
$$
S(L) =
\begin{cases}
\exp\left(-\frac{(L - \mu)^2}{2\sigma_L^2}\right), & L < \mu \\
\exp\left(-\frac{(L - \mu)^2}{2\sigma_R^2}\right), & L \geq \mu
\end{cases}
$$

### Normal
$$
S(L) = \exp\left(-\frac{(L - \mu)^2}{2\sigma^2}\right)
$$

## SPR
$$
SPR = \frac{\text{spawning output under fishing}}{\text{unfished spawning output}}
$$

# Workflow example

## Load package and data

```{r,warning=F,message=F}
library(FLicc)
data("alfonsino")
```

We load the example dataset containing observed length-frequency distributions.

## Define life-history parameters

```{r}
lhpar <- FLPar(
  linf = 55.7,
  k    = 0.08,
  M    = 0.162,
  L50  = 31.1859,
  a    = 0.004721956,
  b    = 3.146168,
  s    = 0.7
)
```

These define growth, mortality, maturity, and weight relationships used in the model.

## Build stock object

```{r}
lfds <- lfd_alfonsino
stklen <- stocklen(lfds, lhpar)
```

This constructs the equilibrium population-at-length representation.

## Inspect observed data

```{r}
plot_lfd(lfds, type = "relmax")
```

We visualize the observed length distributions to understand their structure.

## Fit the model

```{r}
fit <- fiticc(
  lfds,
  stklen,
  sel_fun = c("dsnormal", "logistic"),
  catch_by_gear = c(0.78, 0.22),
  settings = list(CVL=0.1, GL=50, catch.sd=0.05)
)
```

This step:
- builds TMB inputs,
- estimates selectivity and fishing mortality,
- fits predicted to observed compositions.

## Compare observed and fitted distributions

```{r}
plot_plen(fit)
plot_plen(fit, by_gear = TRUE)
```

These plots assess model fit overall and by gear.

## Inspect selectivity

```{r}
matplot(fit$tmb_data$Lmid, fit$report$Sel, type = "l", lwd = 2)
```

This shows estimated selectivity curves.

## Extract SPR

```{r}
fit$report$spr
```

SPR summarizes reproductive capacity under current fishing.

## Multi-year extension

```{r eval=FALSE}
results <- lapply(years, function(y) {
  fit_y <- fiticc(...)
  fit_y$report$spr
})
```

Apply the model across years for comparative analysis.

# Summary

This vignette demonstrated the full workflow and interpretation of results.
